# CalSim View ÔÇö Implementation Reference

> **Maintenance rule**: whenever a module is added/renamed/removed, an API
> endpoint or schema field changes, the data pipeline or artifact formats
> change, or a major frontend component is added/removed ÔÇö **update this file
> in the same session**.

## 1. Repository layout

```
calsim-view/
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ .github/
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ copilot-instructions.md       ÔćÉ agent instructions (build commands, conventions)
Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ memory/
Ôöé       ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ IMPLEMENTATION.md         ÔćÉ this file
Ôöé
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ csview/                           ÔćÉ Python package (backend + CLI tools)
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ geo/                          ÔćÉ GeoSchematic loader + data models
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ models.py                 ÔćÉ GeoNode, GeoArc, GeoNetwork dataclasses
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ loader.py                 ÔćÉ load_from_catalog(), load_from_dir(), _build_geojson()
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ wresl_parser.py           ÔćÉ WRESL define + connectivity parsing
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ diagnostics.py            ÔćÉ topology cross-reference checks
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ __main__.py               ÔćÉ CLI: build catalog.json + network.geojson
Ôöé   Ôöé
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ study/                        ÔćÉ Parquet-backed study results + DSS reader
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ store.py                  ÔćÉ StudyStore + GwBudgetStore (lazy Parquet load)
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ dss_reader.py             ÔćÉ HEC-DSS binary reader (pydsstools wrapper)
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ dss_cache.py              ÔćÉ Pickle cache layer (skip pydsstools on rebuild)
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ gw_budget.py              ÔćÉ GW budget pipeline: crosswalk Ôćĺ DSS Ôćĺ WBA parquet
Ôöé   Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ __main__.py               ÔćÉ CLI: DSS Ôćĺ results.parquet + results_meta.json + gw_budget
Ôöé   Ôöé

Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ study/

Ôöé           ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ results_meta.json     ÔćÉ study name, variable metadata, date range
Ôöé           ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ gw_budget.parquet     ÔćÉ GW budget: 50 WBAs ├Ś 11 C-parts (1 199 rows)
Ôöé           ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ gw_budget_meta.json   ÔćÉ GW budget WBA list, C-part labels, units
Ôöé
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ reference/                        ÔćÉ local-only, gitignored
    ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ geoschematic/                 ÔćÉ source GeoJSON (CalSim3_GeoSchematic_20221227)

            ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ study_meta.json       ÔćÉ study identity + DSS file hints
```

## 2. Architecture overview

```
ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ One-time build steps ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ
Ôöé                                                             Ôöé
Ôöé  GeoSchematic GeoJSON ÔöÇÔöÇÔľ║ python -m csview.geo ÔöÇÔöÇÔľ║ data/network/
Ôöé  WRESL scripts                 (--diagnose --fix-topology)  Ôöé
Ôöé                                                             Ôöé
Ôöé  DSS output files ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔľ║ python -m csview.study ÔöÇÔöÇÔľ║ data/study/<slug>/
Ôöé  catalog.json                                               Ôöé
Ôöé                                                             Ôöé
Ôöé  React sources ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔľ║ npm run build ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔľ║ csview/app/static/
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöś

ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ Runtime ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ
Ôöé                                                             Ôöé
Ôöé  python -m csview.app --network-dir ... --study ...         Ôöé
Ôöé       ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ AppState.load()                                   Ôöé
Ôöé       Ôöé     ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ load_from_catalog() Ôćĺ GeoNetwork (in-memory)Ôöé
Ôöé       Ôöé     ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ StudyStore.from_dir() Ôćĺ lazy Parquet        Ôöé
Ôöé       ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ /api/network/* Ôćĺ network router                   Ôöé
Ôöé       ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ /api/study/*   Ôćĺ study router                     Ôöé
Ôöé       ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ /*             Ôćĺ React SPA (static files)         Ôöé
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöś
```

Key design decisions:

- **Single identifier per feature**: `feature_id` is the canonical key ÔÇö
  `cs3_id.upper()` for nodes, `arc_id.upper()` for arcs.
- **GeoJSON baked at build time**: `network.geojson` is written once and served
  as a static file; never regenerated at runtime.
- **Lazy Parquet loading**: `StudyStore` loads `results.parquet` only on the
  first `/api/study/feature/ÔÇŽ` request; subsequent queries are O(1) column
  lookups.  Parquet timestamps are already end-of-month for the correct
  period ÔÇö no runtime shift is applied.
- **Build order matters**: when rebuilding both, **network first** ÔÇö the study
  builder reads `catalog.json` to decide which DSS variables to extract
  (including `wresl_suggestion` target arc IDs).

---

## 3. Data pipeline ÔÇö one-time builds

### 3a. Network catalog

```bash
python -m csview.geo \
    --geo-dir  reference/geoschematic \
    --wresl    reference/calsim-studies/study_a/Run/System \
    --out      data/network/ \
    --diagnose --fix-topology
```

Reads raw GeoSchematic GeoJSON + WRESL system tables. Writes:

- `catalog.json` ÔÇö node/arc metadata + `variable_to_node` mapping
- `network.geojson` ÔÇö FeatureCollection (arcs as LineStrings, nodes as Points)
- Overlay files: `watersheds.geojson`, `water_budget_areas.geojson`,
  `demand_units.geojson`, `c2vsim_elements.geojson`, `c2vsim_subregions.geojson`
- `network_diagnostics.json` ÔÇö topology issue report (with `--diagnose`)

**`--diagnose`** runs 7 checks against WRESL connectivity equations:

| Check | Kind constant | What it detects |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `arc_no_connectivity` | Geo arc absent from all WRESL equations (193 arcs) |
| 1b | (same) | Delivery arc whose `define` is commented out in WRESL, OR a `D_`/`R_` arc with no geo sibling (e.g. `D_BKR004_NBA009_SCWA_B`) |
| 2 | `arc_no_geo` | WRESL arc absent from GeoJSON (`C_/I_/E_/S_` only, 265) |
| 3 | `from_node_mismatch` | Geo from_node Ôëá WRESL-derived from node (15) |
| 4 | `to_node_mismatch` | Geo to_node Ôëá WRESL-derived to node (85) |
| 5 | `node_no_continuity` | Geo node has no `goal continuity` equation (83) |
| 6 | `node_name_mismatch` | Near-match (Levenshtein ÔëĄ 2) between geo/WRESL node IDs (70) |
| 7 | `arc_endpoint_suggestion` | Geo arc + WRESL arc share `(type_prefix, from_node)` but differ in downstream label (60 pairs Ôćĺ 47 arcs annotated) |

Side effects of `--diagnose`:
- Sets `solver_active=false` on `arc_no_connectivity` arcs **unless** the arc
  has an active WRESL `define` entry in `wresl_cat` (i.e. it is a known solver
  variable whose continuity `goal` is commented out ÔÇö e.g. `C_ORP000`,
  `I_LOSVQ`).  Currently 181 arcs deactivated; 12 kept active via this exemption.
- Populates `missing_arcs` on nodes referenced by `arc_no_geo` arcs (C_/I_/E_/S_ only).
  **Also** adds `D_`/`R_` arcs from connectivity equations that are WRESL-defined
  (in `wresl_cat`) but absent from `gn.arcs` ÔÇö e.g. `D_BKR004_NBA009_SCWA_B`
  alongside its geo sibling `D_BKR004_NBA009`.  Produces 203 additional annotations.
- Populates `inflow_arcs` / `outflow_arcs` on nodes directly from the parsed
  connectivity equations (1,170 nodes).  Arc IDs are uppercase and include
  both geo arcs and SG/phantom arcs.  Used by the study router for
  authoritative direction tagging without data-sign heuristics.
- Builds a global `arc_connectivity: Dict[str, str]` index from **all** node
  equations (including non-geo nodes not stored in `catalog.json` nodes).
  Key = arc_id.upper(), value = `"in"` or `"out"`.  Stored at top level of
  `catalog.json` and loaded into `GeoNetwork.arc_connectivity`.  Used as
  fallback when an SG variable's node name doesn't match the queried node ÔÇö
  e.g. `SG455_CWD009_77` appears in CWD013's equation (a non-geo node) but
  is mapped to CWD009 by name; the global index resolves direction = "in".
- Populates `wresl_suggestion` on arcs matched by check 7 (47 arcs)

**`--fix-topology`** (requires `--diagnose`): patches `from_node`/`to_node`
for mismatched arcs using WRESL-authoritative endpoints (15 from + 85 to).

**Connectivity file discovery**: `parse_connectivity()` uses
`rglob("*.wresl")` filtered by `"connectivity" in name.lower()` to catch all
naming variants. Finds 18 files across sub-basins.

### 3b. Study results

```bash
python -m csview.study \
    --source  reference/calsim-studies/study_a \
    --catalog data/network/catalog.json \
    --out     data/study/study_a/ \
    --cache-dir data/study/study_a/.dss_cache
```

> **Always use `--cache-dir`.** The DSS pickle cache
> (`data/study/study_a/.dss_cache/`) stores every DSS variable as a
> `.dss.pkl` file. With the cache the build takes ~10 s; without it
> `pydsstools` reads the raw DSS files (3ÔÇô5 min). The cache auto-regenerates
> when the DSS file's mtime is newer than the pickle, so `--cache-dir` is
> safe to use unconditionally. **Never omit `--cache-dir`** unless the
> pickle files are suspected to be corrupt (delete them first if so).

Reads DV, GWOUT, and SV DSS files. Matches each variable against:
1. `arc_ids` from catalog (includes `wresl_suggestion` target arc IDs)
2. `<PREFIX><cs3_id>` patterns (34 prefix families, longest-first)

Writes `results.parquet` + `results_meta.json`. Optionally patches
`catalog.json` with `variable_to_node` entries and `node.dss_variables` lists.

If `study_meta.json` exists in `--source`, the builder reads `name`,
`dv_file`, `gwout_file`, `sv_file`, and `simulation_period` from it.

### 3c. GW budget (automatic ÔÇö runs inside study builder)

The study builder auto-discovers `*GroundwaterBudget*.dss` in `DSS/output/`
and the crosswalk file `CVElementsToCalsimRegions*.dat` in
`Run/CVGroundwater/Data/`. When both are found the GW budget pipeline runs
automatically after the main parquet build. Writes:

- `gw_budget.parquet` ÔÇö wide DataFrame: rows = monthly timestamps, columns =
  `{WBA_ID}__{C_PART}` (e.g. `07N__PUMPING`). 50 WBAs ├Ś 11 C-parts = 550 cols.
- `gw_budget_meta.json` ÔÇö WBA IDs, C-part list, human-readable labels, units.

The pipeline reads the crosswalk file to map CalSim SR (groundwater region)
numbers to Water Budget Area (WBA) IDs, then sums DSS values per WBA per
C-part. Exterior-area SRs (e.g. `indxEA_60N`) are folded into their parent
WBA. Uses a dedicated `.gwbudget.pkl` cache (separate from the main DSS cache)
because the GW budget DSS has 11 C-parts per SR that must be kept distinct
(the standard cache merges C-parts).

### 3d. Frontend

```bash
cd csview/app/frontend && npm install && npm run build
```

Compiles React/Vite source into `csview/app/static/`.

---

## 4. Disk artifact reference

### `catalog.json`

```jsonc
{
  "nodes": {
    "SHSTA": {
      "cs3_id": "SHSTA",
      "description": "storage- reservoir or lake",
      "node_type": "Reservoir",
      "lon": -122.41, "lat": 40.72,
      "hydro_region": "SAC",
      "dss_variables": ["S_SHSTA", "E_SHSTA"],   // filled by study builder
      "missing_arcs": ["E_SPICE"],                // WRESL arcs with no geometry (--diagnose)
      "inflow_arcs":  ["C_SPICE", "SG456_SHSTA_78"],  // uppercase; from connectivity (--diagnose)
      "outflow_arcs": ["C_SHSTA_WHL"]                  // uppercase; from connectivity (--diagnose)
    }
  },
  "arcs": {
    "C_FOLSM": {
      "arc_id": "C_FOLSM",
      "name": "American River at Folsom",
      "arc_type": "Channel",
      "from_node": "FOLSM", "to_node": "AMD004",
      "hydro_region": "SAC",
      "units": "CFS", "kind": "CHANNEL",
      "capacity_cfs": 240000,       // WRESL upper bound; null = unbounded
      "solver_active": true,        // false = no WRESL equation (--diagnose)
      "wresl_suggestion": null,     // probable WRESL counterpart (--diagnose)
      "coordinates": [[lon, lat], ...]
    }
  },
  "variable_to_node": {
    "S_SHSTA": "SHSTA",
    "GP_07N_NU": "07N_NU"
  },
  "arc_connectivity": {
    "SG455_CWD009_77": "in",   // direction of arc in its WRESL continuity equation
    "SG456_CWD009_78": "in",   // covers ALL nodes incl. non-geo; 3,173 entries
    "C_CWD009": "out"
  }
}
```

### `network.geojson`

4 138 features (2 602 arcs + 1 536 nodes). Arc properties include
`feature_id`, `feature_kind="arc"`, `arc_type`, `from_node`, `to_node`,
`capacity_cfs`, `solver_active`, `wresl_suggestion`. Node properties include
`feature_id`, `feature_kind="node"`, `node_type`, `missing_arcs`.

> **Invariant**: `feature_kind` must be exactly `"arc"` or `"node"`. The
> frontend branches on this value for styling and behavior.

### `results.parquet`

Wide DataFrame: rows = monthly `DatetimeIndex` (1 200 rows, 1921-10 Ôćĺ 2021-09),
columns = CalSim variable names (~6 026 columns). All TAF variables are
converted to CFS during build except storage and storage-zone (which
remain in TAF).  The `_KEEP_TAF_KINDS` set controls exemptions.

### `results_meta.json`

```jsonc
{
  "study": "danube_hist",
  "built_at": "2026-02-24T...",
  "dv_file": "...", "gwout_file": "...", "sv_file": "...",
  "simulation_period": {"start": "1920-10", "end": "2021-09"},
  "date_range": {"start": "1921-10-31", "end": "2021-09-30"},
  "variables": {
    "C_FOLSM": {"feature_id": "C_FOLSM", "feature_kind": "arc", "units": "CFS", "kind": "CHANNEL"},
    "S_SHSTA": {"feature_id": "SHSTA",   "feature_kind": "node", "units": "TAF", "kind": "STORAGE"}
  },
  "node_dss_variables": {"SHSTA": ["S_SHSTA", "E_SHSTA"]},
  "arc_dss_variables": {"C_SAC000_MIN": ["NDOI_MIN"]}
}
```

### `gw_budget.parquet`

Wide DataFrame: rows = monthly `DatetimeIndex` (1 199 rows, 1921-11 Ôćĺ 2021-09),
columns = `{WBA_ID}__{C_PART}` (550 columns, 50 WBAs ├Ś 11 C-parts). Values
in AC.FT. (acre-feet). WBA IDs use zero-padded two-digit numbers (e.g. `02`,
`07N`, `60S`). 42 WBAs have matching polygons in `water_budget_areas.geojson`;
8 have no polygon (GWR15ÔÇôGWR21 in Tulare Basin + NBAY).

### `gw_budget_meta.json`

```jsonc
{
  "built_at": "2026-02-27T...",
  "units": "AC.FT.",
  "date_range": {"start": "1921-11-30", "end": "2021-09-30"},
  "c_parts": ["CHANGE_STORAGE", "FLOW_BC", "GHB", ...],  // 11 components
  "c_part_labels": {
    "PUMPING": "Pumping", "NET_DEEP_PERC": "Net Deep Percolation", ...
  },
  "wba_ids": ["02", "04", "07N", "60S", "GWR15", ...],  // 50 total
  "wba_ids_with_polygon": ["02", "04", "07N", ...],       // 42 matched
  "wba_ids_without_polygon": ["GWR15", ..., "NBAY"]       // 8 unmatched
}
```

### `study_meta.json` (in source dir)

```jsonc
{
  "name": "danube_hist",
  "description": "...",
  "dv_file":    "DSS/output/DCR2023_DV_9.3.1_Danube_Hist_v1.7.dss",
  "gwout_file": "DSS/output/CVGroundwaterOut.dss",
  "sv_file":    "DSS/input/DCR2023_SV_Danube_Hist_v1.7.dss",
  "gw_budget_file": "DSS/output/CVGroundwaterBudget.dss",
  "gw_crosswalk_file": "Run/CVGroundwater/Data/CVElementsToCalsimRegions_20190927.dat",
  "simulation_period": {"start": "1920-10", "end": "2021-09"}
}
```

Optional fields `gw_budget_file` and `gw_crosswalk_file` allow explicit
paths; when absent, the builder auto-discovers them by glob.

---

## 5. Python package ÔÇö `csview`

### `csview.geo.models`

| Symbol | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `GeoNode` | Dataclass: `cs3_id`, lat/lon, `node_type`, `hydro_region`, `dss_variables`, `missing_arcs: List[str]`, `inflow_arcs: List[str]`, `outflow_arcs: List[str]`. Has `to_dict()` / `from_dict()` classmethods. `inflow_arcs`/`outflow_arcs` hold uppercase arc IDs from WRESL connectivity equations (populated by `--diagnose`). |
| `GeoArc` | Dataclass: `arc_id`, `from_node`, `to_node`, `arc_type`, `coordinates`, `capacity_cfs: Optional[float]`, `solver_active: bool`, `wresl_suggestion: Optional[str]`. Has `to_dict()` / `from_dict()` classmethods. |
| `GeoNetwork` | Container: `nodes`, `arcs`, `geojson`, `variable_to_node` dicts + `lookup_node()`, `lookup_arc()`, `get_feature()` helpers |
| `node_type_from_description()` | Derives node_type from NodeDescription text |
| `ARC_TYPE_MAP` | Dict normalising raw GeoSchematic Type Ôćĺ canonical arc type |

### `csview.geo.loader`

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `load_from_catalog(dir)` | **Runtime**: reads `catalog.json` + `network.geojson` Ôćĺ `GeoNetwork` |
| `load_from_dir(geo_dir, wresl_dir)` | **Build-time**: parses raw GeoJSON, enriches from WRESL |
| `rebuild_geojson(gn)` | Regenerates `gn.geojson` from current node/arc data (call after patching topology) |

### `csview.geo.wresl_parser`

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `parse_system_tables(dir)` | Parses all `*.wresl` `define` statements Ôćĺ `WreslCatalog` |
| `parse_connectivity(dir)` | Parses connectivity-constraint files Ôćĺ `Dict[str, NodeConnectivity]` |

Connectivity file discovery uses `rglob("*.wresl")` filtered by
`"connectivity" in name.lower()` (not a fixed filename glob).

### `csview.geo.diagnostics`

| Symbol | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `run_diagnostics(gn, connectivity, wresl_catalog)` | Orchestrates all 7 checks Ôćĺ `DiagnosticsReport` |
| `_build_wresl_topology()` | Derives from/to node maps from WRESL connectivity equations |
| `_check_arc_no_connectivity()` | Check 1: geo arcs absent from WRESL |
| `_check_delivery_no_define()` | Check 1b: delivery arcs w/ commented-out defines |
| `_check_arc_no_geo()` | Check 2: WRESL arcs absent from geo |
| `_check_endpoint_mismatches()` | Checks 3-4: from/to node mismatches |
| `_check_node_no_continuity()` | Check 5: nodes w/o continuity equation |
| `_check_node_name_typos()` | Check 6: near-match (Levenshtein) node IDs |
| `_check_arc_endpoint_suggestions()` | Check 7: WRESL arc suggestions |
| `DiagnosticsReport` | `issues`, `stats`, `wresl_from`, `wresl_to`, `all_wresl_arcs`, `arc_suggestions` |

### `csview.study.store`

| Method | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `StudyStore.from_dir(dir)` | Constructor; validates parquet exists |
| `.variables` | All variable names (reads Parquet schema, no full load) |
| `.date_range` | `(start, end)` from metadata; Parquet fallback |
| `.get_series(varname)` | Returns `pd.Series` for one variable |
| `.get_feature_series(feature_id)` | All series for a feature (arc: direct match; node: via `node_dss_variables`) |
| `.has_variable(varname)` | O(1) check via cached uppercaseÔćĺcolumn dict |

Internally, `_upper_to_col` dict is built on first load for O(1) case-insensitive column lookups.

### `csview.study.store` ÔÇö `GwBudgetStore`

Separate dataclass for GW budget data, loaded from `gw_budget.parquet` +
`gw_budget_meta.json`.

| Method / Property | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `GwBudgetStore.from_dir(dir)` | Constructor; returns `None` if files missing |
| `.wba_ids` | All WBA IDs (50, incl. unmatched) |
| `.wba_ids_with_polygon` | WBA IDs that match `water_budget_areas.geojson` (42) |
| `.c_parts` | List of C-part names (11) |
| `.c_part_labels` | Human-readable C-part names |
| `.units` | Data units (AC.FT.) |
| `.get_wba_budget(wba_id)` | Returns `{c_part: pd.Series}` for one WBA |
| `.has_wba(wba_id)` | O(1) membership check |

### `csview.study.dss_cache`

Pickle-based cache layer to skip repeated `pydsstools` reads on rebuilds.

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `cache_dss_file(dss_path, cache_dir)` | Reads all paths via pydsstools, writes `<name>.dss.pkl` |
| `load_or_cache_dss(dss_path, cache_dir)` | Returns `(series_map, catalog_index)` from cache (or creates it) |
| `_is_cache_valid(pkl_path, dss_path)` | Checks pickle mtime > DSS mtime |

Pickle payload: `{"source", "cached_at", "catalog": {var: [pathnames]}, "series": {var: pd.Series}}`.
Used by `--cache-dir` CLI option on the study builder.

### `csview.study.__main__` (CLI builder)

Variable matching: `_resolve_feature(varname, arc_ids, cs3_ids)` checks arc_ids
first (direct match), then strips 34 node prefixes longest-first. The `arc_ids`
set includes `wresl_suggestion` target arcs from catalog so their DSS data is
extracted even though they have no GeoSchematic geometry.

**SG seepage variables** (`SG{num}_{node}_{c2vsim_element}`) use a dynamic
prefix that doesn't fit the static prefix table. `_strip_prefix` handles
them with a dedicated regex (`_SG_PREFIX_RE`) that strips `SG{digits}_` and
the trailing C2VSim element number to match the embedded node ID
(e.g. `SG456_CWD009_78` Ôćĺ `CWD009`). `_DLL` (deep-layer leakage) variants
are excluded as duplicates of the base SG variable.

After prefix stripping, `_strip_prefix` applies three fallback strategies:
1. Strip known trailing tags (e.g. `DV`).
2. Strip a trailing `_\d+` suffix for **storage zone** variables
   (e.g. `S_SHSTA_1` -> `SHSTA_1` -> `SHSTA`).
3. Progressively remove leading `_`-separated segments to find an embedded
   node ID (e.g. `WR_ESL010_09_NA` -> `ESL010_09_NA` -> peel `ESL010_`
   -> `09_NA` matches).  This catches ~900 delivery/contract/loss variables
   whose name embeds a source channel before the destination demand node.

**`--cache-dir` is mandatory** ÔÇö always pass it to use the pickle cache layer
(`dss_cache.py`). Without it the builder falls back to pydsstools and takes
3ÔÇô5 min instead of ~10 s.
Helper `_merge_secondary_dss()` consolidates the DV/GWOUT/SV merge pattern.

### `csview.study.gw_budget`

Self-contained pipeline for groundwater budget data: crosswalk parsing, DSS
reading by C-part, WBA-level aggregation, and parquet output.

| Symbol | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `parse_crosswalk(path)` | Reads `CVElementsToCalsimRegions*.dat` Ôćĺ `{SR_name: wba_id}` mapping (66 entries) |
| `_normalize_wba_id(indx_name)` | Converts `indxWBA_7N` Ôćĺ `"07N"`, `indxEA_60N` Ôćĺ `"60N"`, `indxGWR15` Ôćĺ `"GWR15"` |
| `_pad_wba_num(s)` | Zero-pads single-digit WBA numbers (`"2"` Ôćĺ `"02"`) to match GeoJSON IDs |
| `read_gw_budget_by_cpart(dss_path, cache_dir)` | Reads DSS grouped by C-part Ôćĺ `{(SR, cpart): Series}`; uses `.gwbudget.pkl` cache |
| `build_gw_budget(dss_path, crosswalk_path, out_dir, cache_dir, ...)` | Orchestrates full pipeline Ôćĺ writes parquet + meta |
| `CPART_LABELS` | Dict of C-part Ôćĺ human-readable name (11 entries) |
| `_WBA_IDS` | Set of valid WBA IDs from `water_budget_areas.geojson` (42 entries) |

The `.gwbudget.pkl` cache is separate from the standard DSS cache because the
GW budget DSS has 11 C-parts per variable that must be stored distinctly
(the standard cache merges C-parts by keeping only the first).

### `csview.app.state`

`AppState` singleton: `.network` (`GeoNetwork`), `.studies` (`Dict[str, StudyStore]`),
`.gw_budgets` (`Dict[str, GwBudgetStore]`), `.active_study`. Loaded at startup
via `lifespan` context. `get_gw_budget(name)` returns `GwBudgetStore` or `None`.

### `csview.app.schemas`

| Model | Used for |
|---|---|
| `FeatureSummary` | `/api/network/features` list items |
| `NodeDetail` | Feature detail when node (includes `solver_active`, `seepage_vars`) |
| `ArcDetail` | Feature detail when arc (includes `wresl_suggestion`) |
| `NeighborhoodResponse` | Subgraph response |
| `StudyInfo` / `StudyListResponse` | Study listing |
| `FeatureResultSeries` | Time series response (includes `wresl_suggestion_used`) |
| `GwBudgetMeta` | GW budget availability + WBA/C-part metadata |
| `GwBudgetResponse` | Per-WBA budget series for all C-parts |

`FeatureSummary`, `NodeDetail`, and `ArcDetail` have factory classmethods
(`from_node()`, `from_arc()`, `from_geo()`) that construct instances directly
from `GeoNode`/`GeoArc` objects, eliminating manual serialization helpers in
the routers.

---

## 6. Backend ÔÇö FastAPI application

### Startup sequence

1. `python -m csview.app` Ôćĺ `create_app()` Ôćĺ registers routers + lifespan
2. Lifespan: `AppState.load()` ÔÇö loads `GeoNetwork` + creates `StudyStore`s
3. Uvicorn serves requests

### Router: `/api/network` (network.py)

| Method | Path | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/api/network` | Full `network.geojson` |
| GET | `/api/network/features` | `List[FeatureSummary]` (filterable) |
| GET | `/api/network/features/{id}` | `NodeDetail` or `ArcDetail` |
| GET | `/api/network/features/{id}/neighborhood` | `NeighborhoodResponse` |
| GET | `/api/network/regions` | `List[str]` |
| GET | `/api/network/types` | `List[str]` (node types) |
| GET | `/api/network/arc-types` | `List[str]` |
| GET | `/api/network/overlays/{layer}` | Overlay GeoJSON |

### Router: `/api/study` (study.py)

| Method | Path | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/api/study/studies` | `StudyListResponse` |
| POST | `/api/study/studies/{name}/activate` | `{active: name}` |
| GET | `/api/study/studies/{name}/variables` | `List[str]` |
| GET | `/api/study/feature/{id}` | `FeatureResultSeries` |
| GET | `/api/study/variable/{prmname}` | `FeatureResultSeries` |
| GET | `/api/study/gw_budget/meta` | `GwBudgetMeta` |
| GET | `/api/study/gw_budget/{wba_id}` | `GwBudgetResponse` |

**Feature results enrichment** (study router):

- **Node features**: response includes series for every arc connected to the
  node, each carrying `direction: "in"` or `"out"` in metadata.  When an arc
  has no direct DSS data but has a `wresl_suggestion`, the suggestion's series
  is used as fallback (so suggested arcs appear in the node's Arc Flows chart).
- **Arc features with `wresl_suggestion`**: when the arc has no direct DSS
  match, the router falls back to `store.get_feature_series(wresl_suggestion)`
  and sets `wresl_suggestion_used` in the response. The frontend renders an
  amber banner explaining the data source.

### Neighborhood BFS

`get_neighborhood(feature_id, depth)`:

1. Seed from feature (node or arc endpoints)
2. BFS to `depth` hops through arc from/to nodes
3. Filter `rendered_node_ids` = nodes with a real `GeoNode` (excludes phantom
   zone nodes `"02"`, `"03"`)
4. Include only arcs where both endpoints are in `rendered_node_ids`

---

## 7. REST API reference

### GET /api/network
Full `network.geojson` as loaded from disk.

### GET /api/network/features
Query: `feature_kind`, `hydro_region`, `node_type`, `arc_type` (all optional).

### GET /api/network/features/{feature_id}
Case-insensitive. Returns `NodeDetail` or `ArcDetail` or 404.

### GET /api/network/features/{feature_id}/neighborhood
Query: `depth` (int, default 2). Returns `NeighborhoodResponse`.

### GET /api/study/feature/{feature_id}
Query: `study` (optional, defaults to active). Returns `FeatureResultSeries`:

```jsonc
{
  "feature_id": "SHSTA",
  "study": "danube_hist",
  "series": { "S_SHSTA": [["1921-09-30", 4552.1], ...] },
  "metadata": { "S_SHSTA": {"units": "TAF", "kind": "STORAGE"} },
  "wresl_suggestion_used": null    // set when showing fallback data
}
```

Node responses include connected arc series with `direction` in metadata.
`E_` (evaporation) node variables are also tagged `direction="out"` so they
appear in Arc Flows.  `F_` (spill) variables are suppressed in the frontend
via `SUPPRESSED_KINDS`.

### GET /api/study/variable/{prmname}
Query: `study` (optional). Returns `FeatureResultSeries` for one variable.

### GET /api/study/gw_budget/meta
Query: `study` (optional). Returns `GwBudgetMeta`:

```jsonc
{
  "available": true,
  "wba_ids": ["02", "04", ...],          // 50 total
  "wba_ids_with_polygon": ["02", ...],   // 42 matched
  "c_parts": ["CHANGE_STORAGE", ...],    // 11 components
  "c_part_labels": {"PUMPING": "Pumping", ...},
  "units": "AC.FT."
}
```

### GET /api/study/gw_budget/{wba_id}
Query: `study` (optional). Returns `GwBudgetResponse` with all C-part time
series for the given WBA:

```jsonc
{
  "wba_id": "07N",
  "units": "AC.FT.",
  "c_part_labels": {"PUMPING": "Pumping", ...},
  "series": {
    "PUMPING": [["1921-11-30", -12345.6], ...],
    "NET_DEEP_PERC": [["1921-11-30", 8901.2], ...],
    ...
  }
}
```

### GET /api/network/overlays/{layer}
`layer` Ôłł `watersheds`, `water_budget`, `demand_unit`, `c2vsim_elements`, `c2vsim_subregions`.

---

## 8. Frontend ÔÇö React / Leaflet SPA

**Stack**: React 18, Vite 6, Tailwind CSS 4, React-Leaflet 4, Leaflet 1.9,
leaflet-arrowheads 1.4, Recharts 2, TanStack Query 5.

### `App.jsx`
Three-column resizable layout: `[NeighborhoodGraph | drag | NodePanel | drag | Map]`.
The graph panel and node panel each have independent open/close and width
state.  The graph panel appears only when a feature is selected and
`graphOpen` is true; a header toggle button (graph icon + "Graph") lets the
user show/hide it.  Two independent `col-resize` drag handles separate the
three areas.
State: `selectedNode`, `selectedWba`, `flyToFeature`, `activeStudy`, `panelOpen`,
`graphOpen`, `panelWidth`, `graphWidth`.
When `selectedWba` is set, the right panel shows `GwBudgetPanel` instead of
`NodePanel`; the neighbourhood graph is hidden.

### `NetworkMap.jsx`
- GeoJSON loaded once (React Query, cached indefinitely)
- Arc styling by `arc_type` via `ARC_STYLE` table; inactive arcs dashed at 40% opacity
- Node styling by `node_type` via `NODE_STYLE` table
- Flow arrows via `leaflet-arrowheads` (`frequency: "endonly"`)
- Selected feature: yellow highlight + `bringToFront()`
- Legend overlay listing all types with colours

#### Style tables

**Node types**: Reservoir (blue `#3b82f6`), Groundwater (emerald `#10b981`),
Demand-Agricultural (amber `#f59e0b`), Demand-Urban (orange `#f97316`),
Demand-Refuge (lime `#84cc16`), Shortage (red `#ef4444`), Contract (yellow
`#eab308`), Return Flow (lime `#a3e635`), Evaporation (dark slate `#475569`),
Water Quality (cyan `#22d3ee`), Power (purple `#a855f7`), default (gray
`#6b7280`).

**Arc types**: Channel (light blue `#60a5fa`), Diversion (emerald `#34d399`),
Inflow (teal `#2dd4bf`), Return Flow (lime `#a3e635`), Minimum Flow (sky
`#38bdf8`), Surface Runoff (light green `#86efac`), Seepage (slate `#64748b`),
Deep Percolation (gray `#6b7280`), Tile Drain (stone `#78716c`), Groundwater
(green `#059669`), Evaporation (dark slate `#475569`), Spill (pale sky
`#7dd3fc`), Closure Term (gray `#4b5563`), Delta Accretion (violet `#a78bfa`),
Delta Depletion (pink `#f472b6`), default (gray `#4b5563`).

### `RegionalLayers.jsx`
Collapsible toggle panel (top-right of map) for regional polygon overlays.
Five layers: Watersheds, Water Budget Areas, Demand Units, C2VSim Elements,
C2VSim Subregions.  Each layer is fetched lazily via React Query on first
toggle and cached indefinitely.  When any regional layer is active, the
network `GeoJSONLayer` is muted (reduced opacity/weight) so regional
polygons are clearly visible while the schematic stays in context.
Clicking a polygon zooms to its bounds.  Water Budget Area polygons route
clicks to `onWbaSelect` (opening the GW budget panel) instead of the
standard network-select handler.  A badge on the toggle button shows
the count of active layers.

### `NodePanel.jsx`
Feature detail panel with sections:
- **Header**: feature_id, name/description, type badge, close button
- **Information** (consolidated): merges old Properties + Geographic + Arc
  Details into a single collapsible section.  Common fields first (kind,
  solver badge, region), then arc-specific (from/to node, capacity),
  then node-specific (river, gage, lat/lon).  Solver badge shows for both
  arcs and nodes (`solver_active` ÔÇö nodes are active when they have DSS
  variables, missing_arcs, inflow_arcs, or outflow_arcs).  `wresl_suggestion` shown as subtle gray note.
  The Solver row shows:
  - Green **solver active** OR gray **schematic only** badge (per `solver_active`)
  - Yellow **solver-only arcs** pill (when `missing_arcs` non-empty)
  - Indigo **seepage** pill (when `seepage_vars` non-empty)
- **Solver-Only Arcs** (node features): collapsible section showing `missing_arcs`
  (gray tags) and `seepage_vars` (indigo tags). Opens when either is non-empty.
- **Model Results**: `ResultsChart` with date-range slider; gray "No DSS
  output" message when both arc and suggestion lack data

`NodePanel` owns **persistent chart state** (`displayUnit`, `aggMode`) that
survives feature-selection changes.  These are passed to `ResultsChart` as
controlled props (`displayUnit` / `onDisplayUnitChange`, `aggMode` /
`onAggModeChange`).  The year-range slider likewise persists: on feature
change, existing indices are *clamped* to the new years array instead of
reset, so the user's zoom level is preserved.

### `GwBudgetPanel.jsx`
Stacked bar chart (Recharts `BarChart` with `stackOffset="sign"`) showing
groundwater budget components for a selected Water Budget Area. Props:
`wbaId`, `activeStudy`, `onClose`.

- Fetches all 11 C-part series via `fetchGwBudget(wbaId, study)`
- **Aggregation modes**: Time Series (monthly) or Water Year (annual sum)
- **Toggleable legend**: click a component name to hide/show its bars
- **Custom tooltip**: lists per-component values + net total
- **Color scheme**: warm tones for outflows (PUMPING = red `#ef4444`),
  cool tones for inflows (NET_DEEP_PERC = blue `#3b82f6`)
- **Chart layout**: positive bars stack upward (recharge), negative stack
  downward (extraction); a zero reference line separates them

### `ResultsChart.jsx`
`splitSeries()` partitions variables into four buckets:

| Bucket | Criteria | Chart |
|---|---|---|
| `arcFlows` | has `direction` in metadata | `StackedFlowChart` |
| `storage` | kind Ôłł STORAGE/GW-STORAGE/GROUNDWATER | `LineChartPanel` |
| `storageZones` | kind = STORAGE-ZONE | `LineChartPanel` (as zone areas) |
| `balance` | everything else | `LineChartPanel` |

**StackedFlowChart**: outflows negated, stacked areas + lines, CFS/TAF toggle.
Channels with reverse (negative) flow are handled via a split-stack approach:
each key's value is clamped to its expected sign for monotone stacking, while
the reverse-sign portion is placed in the *opposite* stack as a synthetic
`_stk_rev_` / `_top_rev_` key (lighter fill, dashed outline).  This keeps
lines from crossing while making reverse flows visible.  Uses `flowSeriesColor()`
for separate positive (cool-tone) / negative (warm-tone) color palettes.
**LineChartPanel**: y-axis anchored to 0, dashed average line, CFS/TAF toggle.
Accepts `isStorage` prop: when true and in annual mode, uses
`aggregateWaterYearEnd` (September-only snapshot) instead of the default
water-year aggregation.  Accepts `useBarForAnnual` prop: when true and in
annual mode, renders a `BarChart`/`Bar` instead of `LineChart`/`Line`.
Bar charts are enabled for the **Storage** panel and the **arc "Flow"**
panel (few series).  All non-storage TAF variables are converted to CFS
at build time, so the **"Other"** chart displays a consistent CFS unit.
Accepts optional `zoneSeries` prop: when
provided and in raw time-series mode, renders storage-zone boundaries as
light stacked `Area` fills behind the main storage line.  Zone keys
(e.g. `S_SHSTA_1`ÔÇô`S_SHSTA_6`) are sorted numerically and stacked
bottom-up using `ZONE_COLORS` palette.  Zone areas are hidden in
monthly-average and annual aggregation modes.

**Aggregation modes** (all charts): an `AggToggle` row offers three modes:
- **Time Series** (`raw`): default raw monthly data
- **Monthly Avg** (`monthly`): 12 rows (OctÔÇôSep water-year order), averaged
  across the selected date window
- **Water Year** (`annual`): one row per water year ÔÇö **TAF volumes are
  summed**, **CFS rates are averaged** (via `avgKeys` parameter to
  `aggregateWaterYear`), and **storage** uses the September 30 snapshot
  (`aggregateWaterYearEnd`)

Shared helpers live in extracted modules:
- **`charts/chartUtils.js`**: `COLORS`, `REF_COLORS`, `seriesColor()`,
  `POS_COLORS` (8 cool-tone), `NEG_COLORS` (8 warm-tone),
  `flowSeriesColor(key, isNegative, orderIdx)`,
  `dateToWaterYear(dateKey)`, `shortKey()`, `daysInMonth()`, `cfsToTaf()`,
  `buildChartData()` (filters by water year, not calendar year),
  `splitSeries()` (returns `arcFlows`, `storage`, `storageZones`, `balance`),
  `WB_TERMS`, `WB_ORDER`, `WB_COLORS`, `WB_POSITIVE`,
  `ZONE_COLORS` (6-color palette for storage zone bands),
  `computeYearTicks()`, `aggregateMonthlyAvg(data, keys)`,
  `aggregateWaterYear(data, keys, avgKeys=null)` (avgKeys = set of CFS
  rate keys to average instead of sum),
  `aggregateWaterYearEnd(data, keys)`
- **`charts/ChartParts.jsx`**: `ToggleLegend`, `ChartTooltip`
  (tooltip preserves negative sign in values; accepts optional `excludeKeys`
  set to suppress zone keys from tooltip display)

### `YearRangeSlider.jsx`
Dual-thumb range slider (~160 lines) with drought preset pills and
pan/shift-click support.  Operates on **water years** (WY) derived in
`NodePanel.jsx`.  Labels display "WY YYYY" format.

### `constants/mapStyles.js`
Single source of truth for node/arc visual styles: `NODE_STYLE`,
`DEFAULT_NODE_STYLE`, `nodeColor()`, `ARC_STYLE`, `DEFAULT_ARC_STYLE`.
Imported by `NetworkMap.jsx` and `NeighborhoodGraph.jsx`.

### Other components
- **`NeighborhoodGraph.jsx`**: D3-style force layout SVG of local subgraph.
  Depth selector offers 1ÔÇô4 hops.  Edge arcs are clickable (invisible wider
  hit-area + cursor pointer).  Accepts `onEdgeClick` and `onClose` props.
  Close button (`├Ś`) in the panel header
- **`NodeSearch.jsx`**: autocomplete filtering on feature_id + description
- **`StudySelector.jsx`**: active study dropdown
- **`api/client.js`**: fetch wrappers; `get(path)` throws on non-2xx

---

## 9. Running the application

### First-time full build

```bash
# 1. Network (fast, ~10s)
python -m csview.geo \
    --geo-dir reference/geoschematic \
    --wresl   reference/calsim-studies/study_a/Run/System \
    --out     data/network/ \
    --diagnose --fix-topology

# 2. Study results (~10s with cache; always use --cache-dir)
python -m csview.study \
    --source  reference/calsim-studies/study_a \
    --catalog data/network/catalog.json \
    --out     data/study/study_a/ \
    --cache-dir data/study/study_a/.dss_cache

# 3. Frontend
cd csview/app/frontend && npm install && npm run build
```

### Start server

```bash
python -m csview.app \
    --network-dir data/network/ \
    --study data/study/study_a
```

Options: `--host`, `--port`, `--reload`, `--verbose`. Multiple `--study` flags
for multi-study mode with `--default-study`.

### Current study

`danube_hist` (slug: `study_a`): ~6 026 variables ├Ś 1 200 monthly time steps
(1921-10 Ôćĺ 2021-09). Includes ~356 SG (stream-groundwater seepage) node
variables matched via the `SG{num}_` dynamic prefix.
